data.title: Indian Startup Sarvam Develops AI Model for Domestic Market
data.description: Indian startup Sarvam has introduced an AI model designed to better cater to the languages and cultures of India's vast market. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Sarvam's ambition to offer a competitive alternative to existing models like ChatGPT and Claude. The company aims to address the unique linguistic and cultural needs of the Indian population, positioning its AI as a more localized solution. Sarvam's initiative reflects the growing demand for technology that resonates with regional characteristics, potentially setting a new standard in AI development within the country.

data.title: South Korea Regrets Civilian Drone Incursions into North Korea
data.description: South Korea has officially expressed regret to North Korea following a series of drone incursions conducted by civilians. Bloomberg posted on X that these incidents have heightened tensions between the two nations. The South Korean government emphasized its commitment to preventing such occurrences in the future and maintaining peace on the Korean Peninsula. The drone activities, which were not sanctioned by the South Korean government, have been a point of contention, prompting diplomatic communications between the two countries. South Korea is taking steps to address the situation and ensure that similar incidents do not recur, aiming to uphold regional stability.

data.title: ORCA Surges 50% Amid Strong Investor Demand and Utility Growth
data.description: ORCA's price surged by 50% following the purchase of approximately $1.23 million worth of tokens from exchanges within a 24-hour period, indicating robust investor interest. According to NS3.AI, this rally is supported by enhanced utility metrics, including a 100% year-over-year increase in USDC total value locked. Despite the positive momentum, early profit-taking has led to a slight pullback from the intraday high. Concerns remain regarding token supply control and governance transparency due to the ongoing mint authority held by the owner's wallet.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | U.S. Air Force Unveils New Presidential Aircraft Design
data.description:
The U.S. Air Force announced on February 17 that its executive transport fleet, including the next-generation 'Air Force One' presidential aircraft, will adopt a new color scheme featuring red, white, gold, and deep blue tones. According to Jin10, the exact proportions of these colors remain unclear. The new design replaces the blue and white scheme used since John F. Kennedy's presidency and reintroduces the red, white, and blue palette proposed by U.S. President Donald Trump during his first term.

Reuters reported that Trump's color scheme was previously considered by the Air Force to potentially cause overheating issues due to the dark colors. In 2022, the Biden administration deemed the proposal likely to increase engineering work, time, and costs, leading to its rejection. However, the U.S. Air Force has not detailed how the new design addresses the heat dissipation concerns.

data.title: STOCKS | Dubai Stocks Experience Strongest Start in 12 Years
data.description: Dubai's stock market is witnessing its most robust beginning in over a decade, driven by investor confidence beyond the fluctuations in oil and artificial intelligence sectors. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the market's resilience amid global economic uncertainties. This surge reflects a broader investor sentiment that is focusing on long-term growth prospects rather than short-term market volatility. The positive momentum in Dubai's financial markets underscores a shift in investment strategies, as stakeholders seek stability and sustainable returns in a rapidly changing economic landscape.

data.title: Analysis: Ethereum is currently in a "narrative vacuum," with institutional funding and privacy models becoming key variables for the future.
data.description: Industry insiders believe that Ethereum is currently in a "gap period between narratives," where the market tends to price uncertainty when a clear long-term value logic is lacking. In the past few years, the mainstream narrative has been that scaling primarily occurs at Layer 2, while the mainnet maintains security, decentralization, and a streamlined architecture. This path successfully attracted capital and development resources. However, the current market focus is shifting towards zero-knowledge technology and privacy capabilities returning to the base layer, leading to misaligned expectations among some investors who built valuation models based on the old approach. Complete on-chain transparency presents a real obstacle for institutional funds; large enterprises or funds are unwilling to have their trading strategies tracked in real time. If Ethereum hopes to attract trillions of dollars in institutional funds, protocol-level privacy capabilities will become a key competitive variable. Furthermore, the expansion of the options market around spot ETFs is also changing Ethereum's price formation mechanism, including the increasing influence of covered call strategies and market maker hedging behavior. (Forbes)

data.title: The International Monetary Fund issued a triple warning to Japan.
data.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) released a report on February 17, warning the Japanese government to maintain the independence of the Bank of Japan (BOJ), control fiscal expansion, and avoid addressing livelihood issues by cutting the consumption tax. This report coincided with the Japanese prime ministerial election. The market is closely watching whether Sanae Takaichi will oppose further interest rate hikes by the BOJ, and her previous two-year commitment to "zero out the food consumption tax." Regarding monetary policy, the IMF pointed out that maintaining the BOJ's independence and credibility helps stabilize inflation expectations, and stated that the BOJ "should continue to exit monetary easing, bringing the policy rate to a neutral level by 2027." Regarding fiscal policy, the IMF believes that further easing is not advisable in the short term. This contradicts Takaichi's proposed "responsible and proactive fiscal policy." The IMF believes that while Japan currently has some fiscal space, it still needs to maintain fiscal restraint to solidify its fiscal buffer and maintain its ability to cope with shocks. The IMF predicts that in the long term, Japan's fiscal deficit will widen, spending pressure will increase, and total public debt will further grow. (Jinshi)

data.title: 10x Research: 55%-75% of IBIT's $61 billion in assets are held by market makers and arbitrage hedge funds.
data.description: 10x Research published an article on its X platform stating that despite Bitcoin's 46% drop, the Bitcoin ETF only recorded a net outflow of $8.5 billion. According to the latest Q4 2025 13F filing, it is estimated that 55%-75% of IBIT's $61 billion in assets are held by market makers and arbitrage-focused hedge funds, whose positions are mostly hedging or market-neutral rather than expressing directional views. In Q4 2025, as Bitcoin consolidated around $88,000, market makers reduced their exposure by approximately $1.6 billion to $2.4 billion, reflecting a decline in speculative demand and a reduction in demand for arbitrage inventory.

data.title: Japan's 221st Special Diet Session Opens with New Prime Minister
data.description: Japan's 221st Special Diet session commenced today, marking a significant political event following the recent House of Representatives election. According to Ming Pao, Sanae Takaichi, the 64-year-old president of the Liberal Democratic Party, was elected as the 105th Prime Minister during the House of Representatives plenary session.

data.title: Bitcoin Bear Market Continues, Analyst Willy Woo Explains Three Phases
data.description:
Renowned analyst Willy Woo has indicated that the bear market trend for Bitcoin is still ongoing. According to Odaily, Woo outlines the bear market in three distinct phases.

The first phase marks the onset, where Bitcoin's liquidity collapses, a situation that occurred in the third quarter of 2025, leading to a price decline. As a small-scale asset, Bitcoin is highly sensitive to liquidity changes, often leading the global macroeconomic market into a bear phase months in advance. During this stage, die-hard bulls may claim it's a market correction within a bull run, but they lack concrete evidence of capital inflow, resorting to storytelling instead.

The second phase involves the global stock market entering a bear market. This massive $100 trillion entity moves slowly, akin to a giant supertanker. This represents the mid-stage of Bitcoin's bear market, where all risk assets are declining, confirming the bear market status.

The third phase is when the first signs of recovery appear. Liquidity begins to improve, capital outflows peak and stabilize, and investors start returning. The final price drop typically occurs in this phase, either just before or shortly after the peak of capital outflows.

Currently, within this bear market framework, Bitcoin is in the first phase and is about to transition into the second phase.

data.title: Battery Ventures Secures $3.25 Billion for Technology Investments
data.description: Battery Ventures, a global investment firm, has successfully raised $3.25 billion to support new technology deals. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the firm's focus on investing in innovative tech companies. This substantial funding will enable Battery Ventures to expand its portfolio and capitalize on emerging opportunities within the technology sector. The firm aims to leverage its resources to drive growth and innovation in various tech industries, positioning itself as a key player in the market. Battery Ventures' strategic approach to investment underscores its commitment to fostering technological advancements and supporting promising ventures.

data.title: STOCKS | Japanese Stocks Rebound After Four-Day Decline
data.description: Japanese stocks experienced a rebound following a four-day losing streak. According to RTHK, the Nikkei Index rose by as much as 1.5%, reaching a high of 57,392 points. It closed at 57,143 points, marking an increase of 577 points or 1.02%.

data.title: Long-Term Bitcoin Holders Increase Selling on Exchanges
data.description:
According to data shared by CryptoQuant, Bitcoin’s correction is increasingly affecting long-term holders (LTHs), with on-chain metrics showing a notable shift in behavior. Bitcoin continues to trade more than 45% below its previous all-time high, and recent data suggests that even historically resilient investors are beginning to realize losses and increase exchange activity.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/long-term-bitcoin-holders-increase-selling-on-exchanges/

data.title: Willy Woo: Bitcoin bear market is about to enter its second phase; improved liquidity still needs to wait.
data.description: Renowned analyst Willy Woo states, "I have bad news for those who are perpetually bullish on Bitcoin: the bear market trend is still ongoing and should be divided into three phases: Phase One – The Beginning: Bitcoin liquidity has collapsed, a situation that occurred in the third quarter of 2025, and prices begin to fall. Bitcoin, as a small-cap asset, is extremely sensitive to liquidity. This is why it often leads the global macroeconomy into a bear market, usually months in advance. In other words, Bitcoin reacts quickly when savvy money withdraws. In this phase, die-hard bulls blindly claim it's a correction within the bull market, but they offer no concrete evidence of capital inflows, only fabricated stories. Phase Two – Global Stock Markets Turn Bearish: This is a behemoth worth a staggering $100 trillion, like a giant supertanker – moving slowly. This is the mid-stage of the Bitcoin bear market; all risk assets are falling, and there's no doubt we're in a bear market. Phase Three – The Dawn: In this phase, liquidity begins to improve, capital outflows peak and stabilize. Investors are returning." The final price crash typically occurs at this stage, possibly before or shortly after the peak of capital outflows. Within the current bear market framework, Bitcoin is currently in the first stage and poised to enter the second.

data.title: U.S. Public Support for Trump's Immigration Policy Hits New Low
data.description: A recent poll indicates that public support for U.S. President Donald Trump's immigration policy has reached its lowest point. According to Jin10, this development comes as Tricia McLaughlin, the Assistant Secretary for Public Affairs at the Department of Homeland Security, announced her resignation on February 17. The decline in support reflects growing dissatisfaction among Americans with the current administration's approach to immigration.

data.title: Glencore Reports Decline in Annual Profit Amid Coal Earnings Drop
data.description: Glencore has announced a decrease in its full-year profit, attributing the decline to reduced earnings from its coal operations despite record copper prices. Bloomberg posted on X that the company's financial performance was impacted by the downturn in coal, which could not be balanced by the gains in copper. The mining giant's results highlight the challenges faced in its coal sector, which have overshadowed the benefits of high copper prices. The report underscores the volatility in the commodities market and the difficulties in maintaining profitability across different sectors.

data.title: Bitcoin ETF trimmed as Harvard adds $86.8M Ethereum ETF
data.description:
Harvard Management Company trimmed its Bitcoin ETF by ~21% and initiated an $86. 8M Ethereum ETF stake; analysts cite rebalancing and measured positioning.
source: https://coincu.com/news/bitcoin-etf-trimmed-as-harvard-adds-86-8m-ethereum-etf/

data.title: Nvidia Expands AI Initiatives in India with New Partnerships
data.description:
Nvidia is intensifying its focus on India through a range of local partnerships and infrastructure investments aimed at boosting the country's artificial intelligence capabilities. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Nvidia's strategic move to support India's growing tech landscape.

The company is collaborating with Indian firms to enhance AI infrastructure, which is expected to play a crucial role in the nation's technological advancement. These partnerships are part of Nvidia's broader strategy to tap into India's burgeoning market and contribute to its digital transformation.

By investing in local initiatives, Nvidia aims to foster innovation and support the development of AI technologies in India. This move aligns with the country's ambitions to become a global leader in AI and digital solutions.

Nvidia's commitment to India reflects the increasing importance of the region in the global tech industry, as companies seek to leverage local talent and resources to drive growth and innovation.

data.title: Indian Central Bank's Proposal to Tighten Control on Offshore Rupee Transactions
data.description: The Reserve Bank of India's recent proposal mandates banks to report their offshore rupee transactions, a move analysts believe will strengthen the central bank's control over the currency market. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this initiative is part of a broader strategy to monitor and regulate currency flows more effectively. Analysts suggest that this measure could lead to increased transparency and oversight in the foreign exchange market, potentially impacting the rupee's volatility and liquidity. The proposal reflects the central bank's ongoing efforts to stabilize the currency and ensure a more controlled economic environment.

data.title: Peter Thiel Sells Entire Stake in Ethereum Treasury Firm ETHZilla
data.description: Billionaire investor Peter Thiel has completely divested his holdings in the Ethereum treasury firm ETHZilla. Crypto KOL Ted posted on X. The reasons behind Thiel's decision to exit ETHZilla remain unclear, but there is speculation that he might be shifting his focus to other ventures, such as Bitmine. Thiel's move has sparked discussions within the cryptocurrency community about potential strategic shifts in his investment portfolio.
